_meta.portal: https://digitap.ai (docs delivered as PDFs; no self-serve portal scraped)
_meta.compiledBy: Claude Code — extracted from the 8 Digitap API-doc PDFs in the 'production credentials' hand-off (KYC v4.7, Employment v2.3, Credit Analytics v2.7, Location v1.3, Email v1, Face-Match v1.2, KYC-OCR v1.1, FL Web SDK v1.4)

production.note: KYC-validation + Employment(/cv) + Email(/cv) live under svc.digitap.ai; Credit-Analytics, Location(/ent), Face-Match(/fmfl) and KYC-OCR(/ocr) live under api.digitap.ai. Each API record carries its own 'host'.

auth.note: client_id and client_secret are issued by Digitap on registration and DIFFER between UAT and production. NOT an opaque token (that is Signzy). No real credentials were present in the doc hand-off — every value in the PDFs is a placeholder.

rules: All endpoints are POST + application/json, except the FL Web SDK (client-side JS) and any GET noted per-API.
Two production hosts: svc.digitap.ai and api.digitap.ai — read each API's 'host' field.
Employment endpoints are <svc.digitap.ai/cv>/vN/... — the 'path' already includes the /cv prefix.
Async two-step flows (EPFO Employee Search, EPFO Passbook, Liveness SDK) put both call paths in 'path' separated by ' + '.

requestSample values for KYC/Face-Match/OCR were reconstructed from the doc's field tables (those PDFs give tables, not literal JSON request bodies); response structures are copied from the docs, long arrays/base64/report bodies abbreviated with a note. No fields were invented.
This JSON is the machine-readable single source of truth; if DigitapGuide.md disagrees, this JSON wins.

apis.name: PAN Basic (V1)
apis.titleInDocs: KYC - PAN Basic Validation API (V1)
apis.summary: Validates basic details of PAN card holder including PAN number and name. Fetches name from PAN government database and verifies if name matches exactly with NSDL database records.

requestFields.name: client_ref_num
requestFields.desc: Unique ID for this request from the Client side [Max of 45 chars]

requestFields.name: pan
requestFields.desc: PAN Number to be authenticated. Pattern: ^([a-zA-Z]{3})([abcfghljpteABCFGHLJPTE]{1})([a-zA-Z]{1})([0-9]{4})([a-zA-Z]{1})$

requestFields.name: name
requestFields.desc: Exact name as per PAN. Only English characters and special characters ~, ^, /, \, . and & allowed. Max length: 85

requestFields.name: name_match_method
requestFields.desc: Either `fuzzy` or `exact` or `dg_name_match`. Default: `fuzzy`

apis.responseFields: result.{pan, status, name, pan_display_name, seeding_status, name_validated, name_match, name_match_score}

statusCodes.400: One or more parameters format is wrong
statusCodes.404: Path not found / Method not allowed
statusCodes.422: Digitap is unable to fetch the response at this moment

apis.name: UAN based Basic Employment Verification V1
apis.titleInDocs: UAN based Basic Employment Verification Version 1 (UAN-Basic V1) API (DEPRECATED)
apis.summary: DEPRECATED — Digitap recommends V2 or V3 (same input/output shape, better coverage). Maps an individual (via mobile or PAN) to linked EPFO UAN number(s) and returns the recent employer/employment details plus basic KYC fields (name, DOB, gender) sourced from EPFO records. Base host: https://svc.digitap.ai/cv (prod) / https://svcdemo.digitap.work/cv (UAT).

requestFields.name: client_ref_num
requestFields.desc: Unique client-side request id, max 45 chars

requestFields.name: mobile
requestFields.desc: Conditionally mandatory — one of mobile or uan is required

requestFields.name: pan
requestFields.desc: Optional additional lookup input when mobile is given; source of matched UANs is then reported per-UAN in uan_source

requestFields.name: uan
requestFields.desc: Conditionally mandatory — one of mobile or uan is required

requestFields.name: employer_name
requestFields.desc: Used to compute employer_name_match

requestFields.name: employee_name
requestFields.desc: Used to compute employee_name_match

apis.responseFields: result.{uan[], summary.{recent_employer_data.{member_id,establishment_id,date_of_exit,date_of_joining,establishment_name,employer_confidence_score,matching_uan}, is_employed, employee_name_match, employer_name_match, uan_count, date_of_exit_marked}, uan_details.{<uan>: {basic_details.{gender,date_of_birth,employee_confidence_score,name,mobile,aadhaar_verification_status}, employment_details.{member_id,establishment_id,date_of_exit,date_of_joining,leave_reason,establishment_name,employer_confidence_score}}}, uan_source[].{uan,source}}; input_data echoes the request

uan_details._note: 3 additional uan_details entries omitted (one per remaining UAN in result.uan[])

statusCodes.200: OK — request accepted; result_code in body indicates verification outcome
statusCodes.400: One or more parameters format is wrong or missing
statusCodes.505: Source is busy or unavailable, try again later
statusCodes.506: No result found in source — do not retry
statusCodes.result_code_102: Invalid id number or combination of inputs
statusCodes.result_code_103: No record found for the given input
statusCodes.result_code_104: Too many responses (mobile linked to >5 UANs)

apis.name: EPFO Employee Search API
apis.titleInDocs: EPFO Employee Search API
apis.summary: Async two-step flow: the request call (`/v1/employee_name_search/request`) initiates a search for whether a given employee_name appears in EPFO PF-filing records across all establishments of a given company (identified by employer_name or CIN) for the last 3 months or a client-specified list of up to 3 month_year values, and returns only a request_id (no result yet). The status call (`/v1/employee_name_search/status`, keyed on that request_id) then returns the actual name-match report — establishment details, top name matches with confidence scores, PF-filing wage aggregates, and derived booleans (is_employed, is_name_unique, is_recent, etc.) — or a 202-style 'in progress' message if still processing. Base host: https://svc.digitap.ai/cv.

apis.name: EPFO Passbook Extraction API
apis.titleInDocs: EPFO Passbook Extraction API
apis.summary: OTP-gated two-step flow to retrieve an individual's EPF passbook(s) by UAN. Step 1 (`/uan_passbook/get_otp`, keyed by mobile or UAN, PAN optional) triggers an OTP to the UAN-registered mobile and returns a txn_id. Step 2 (`/uan_passbook/get_passbook`, txn_id + otp) returns per-establishment member/employment details and the full month-wise contribution passbook (employee/employer EPF+EPS credits/debits); passing epf_balance=true on step 1 additionally returns net PF balance (employee/employer share breakdown) per establishment and an overall_pf_balance summary — the closest proxy in this suite to actual monthly wage/contribution trends, since EPF contribution amounts are derived from the employee's basic wage. Base host: https://svc.digitap.ai/cv.

apis.name: Credit Analytics Request API

apis.summary: Pulls an Experian bureau/CIR credit report (with CAIS tradelines, CAPS enquiry summary, and the Experian BureauScore) for a mobile number, with optional OTP-based consent, name-lookup, and PAN/DOB/email matching. Host: https://api.digitap.ai/ (prod) or https://apidemo.digitap.work/ (UAT).

requestFields.name: client_ref_num
requestFields.desc: Unique client-side request ID, max 45 chars

requestFields.name: mobile_no
requestFields.desc: 10 numerical digits; regex ^(?:\+91\s?|0)?[6-9]\d{9}$

requestFields.name: name_lookup
requestFields.desc: 1 = use Digitap's name-lookup service to fetch name from mobile number; 0 = not needed

requestFields.name: first_name
requestFields.desc: Required if name_lookup is not 1; e.g. 'Sukhjinder'. Do not pass if name_lookup=1

requestFields.name: last_name
requestFields.desc: Required if name_lookup is not 1; e.g. 'Singh'. Do not pass if name_lookup=1

requestFields.name: date_of_birth
requestFields.desc: YYYY-MM-DD, e.g. 1990-05-10

requestFields.name: pan
requestFields.desc: Regex ^[A-Za-z]{5}\d{4}[A-Za-z]{1}$

requestFields.name: consent_message
requestFields.desc: Explicit end-user consent text authorizing Experian to pull the credit report; must match regex requiring words I/authorize/Experian/Credit/Report

requestFields.name: consent_acceptance
requestFields.desc: yes/no

requestFields.name: device_type
requestFields.desc: web or mobile

requestFields.name: otp
requestFields.desc: OTP given as consent proof, max 6 chars; must be collected client-side and passed here; cannot be reused across transactions

requestFields.name: timestamp
requestFields.desc: DDMMYYYY-HH:MM:SS; must be within ~1 month of request date

requestFields.name: device_ip
requestFields.desc: IP address of the requesting device

requestFields.name: device_id
requestFields.desc: Required only if device_type=mobile; the device IMEI number

requestFields.name: report_type
requestFields.desc: Output format selector: 0=JSON only (default), 1=XML only (pre-signed URL), 2=JSON+XML, 3=JSON+PDF (pre-signed URL), 4=JSON+PDF+XML

requestSample.consent_message: I hereby authorize Experian to pull my credit report for test purpose

SCORE FIELD: result.result_json.INProfileResponse.SCORE.BureauScore (string numeric, e.g. "800"; Experian score) — sibling key BureauScoreConfidLevel usually null.

CreditProfileHeader.Subscriber_Name: Bureau Disclosure Report with Customized Match V3

additional identity fields omitted, all null in sample

CAIS_Account_DETAILS.CAIS_Account_History: [array of {Year,Month,Days_Past_Due,Asset_Classification} — one per month, up to 36 months, abbreviated]
CAIS_Account_DETAILS.CAIS_Holder_Details: [array up to 9, e.g. {Surname_Non_Normalized:'SHUBHRA DUTTA', Gender_Code:'2', Date_of_birth:'19980201'}]
CAIS_Account_DETAILS.note: 1 tradeline shown in sample; real reports may contain up to ~400 (tradeline count >400 triggers a 422 error)

200_call_masked_api.message: please call the masked mobile report API with the following actual mobile numbers: [79XXXXX603, 95XXXXX817].

statusCodes.200_result_code_102: no record found / OR mobile not authenticated in bureau (message instructs caller to invoke Masked Mobile Report API with returned masked numbers)
statusCodes.200_result_code_103: name not found against mobile number
statusCodes.400: validation errors — bad report_type; invalid consent_message; invalid consent_acceptance; reused OTP ('please provide a valid OTP'); invalid device_ip; both OTP and IP invalid; invalid timestamp; one or more key format wrong
statusCodes.401: client authentication failed (bad Basic Auth) OR name look-up service not enabled for the client; also 'Invalid Encryption' (JWE decrypt failure) when encryption is used
statusCodes.422: trade line count exceeds limit (>400 tradelines)
statusCodes.451: Misconfiguration — encryption not enabled for the client (encryption mode only)
statusCodes.503: source error (Experian-side issue; do not retry)

apis.name: FL Web SDK (Selfie Capture + Face Liveness)
apis.titleInDocs: Digitap – FL WEB SDK Guide
apis.summary: SDK integration guide, not a plain REST endpoint. Host: https://api.digitap.ai (Production) / https://apidemo.digitap.work (UAT). Backend calls POST /selfie/v1/initiate to get a hosted redirectionUrl; the user is redirected into the SDK to capture a liveness selfie (instruction screen → capture screen → success screen), then redirects back to the client's clientUiCallback URL with event/success/transactionId query params (SELFIE_CAPTURED, CAPTURE_FAILED, or CUSTOMER_EXIT). The backend then calls POST /selfie/v1/get-selfie-data (or receives a webhook) to fetch the captured image + face-liveness result. Auth header is 'ent_authorization' (Base64 of client_id:client_secret), not the usual 'authorization'.

requestFields.name: uniqueId
requestFields.desc: Unique ID identifying the user/session; used on both the initiate call and (optionally) the get-selfie-data call.

requestFields.name: clientUiCallback
requestFields.desc: UI callback URL the SDK redirects to on completion, with query params event/success/transactionId.

requestFields.name: expiryTime
requestFields.desc: Numeric value in minutes; the generated redirectionUrl expires after this time (else stays active until the user completes capture).

requestFields.name: transactionId
requestFields.desc: Transaction id returned by /selfie/v1/initiate; used to fetch selfie data via /selfie/v1/get-selfie-data. Either uniqueId or transactionId is mandatory for that call.

apis.responseFields: initiate (POST /selfie/v1/initiate): code, model{transactionId,redirectionUrl}, msg. getSelfieData (POST /selfie/v1/get-selfie-data): code, model{status(INITIATED|SUCCESS|FAILED),imageUrl,base64Image,flResult}, msg. webhook (if configured): uniqueId, transactionId, status(SUCCESS|FAILED).

statusCodes.400: unique id cannot be empty / ent authorization cannot be empty / required fields are missing
statusCodes.500: Other errors, or (get-selfie-data) invalid transaction id or unique id found
